Social Networks in R

These data frames have been previously prepared, based on the data from 50 influential people in Seattle tech to follow on Twitter. The file can be seen here, and includes 3 sheets:

  • Network as two columns representing edges.
  • Network as adjacency matrix.
  • Attributes of the Network nodes.

I. Getting Ready:

  • Libraries needed
# Returns TRUE if installed, FALSE if not
# 1. Define the vector of packages you want to check
packages_to_check <- c("igraph","ggplot2","ggraph","rio")

# 2. Check all of them at once
sapply(packages_to_check, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)
##  igraph ggplot2  ggraph     rio 
##    TRUE    TRUE    TRUE    TRUE
###if needed

# install.packages(c('igraph','ggraph'))
  • Set seed
set.seed(123)

II. Network Creation from dataframes

rm(list = ls())
##
fileLink='https://github.com/DACSS-CSSmeths-summer/datafiles/raw/main/seattleTop.xlsx'

edges=rio::import(fileLink, which = 'edges') 
adjacency=rio::import(fileLink, which = 'adjacency')
attributes=rio::import(fileLink,which ="attributes")

Use the previous dataframes to create the network:

  • From the edges:
library(igraph) # package needed
## 
## Attaching package: 'igraph'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     decompose, spectrum
## The following object is masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     union
EliteNet=graph_from_data_frame(edges, directed = F)

summary(EliteNet)
## IGRAPH 26a3ff2 UN-- 46 393 -- 
## + attr: name (v/c)
  • From adjacency matrix:
#prepare matrix
row.names(adjacency)=adjacency[,1] #first column for index
adjacency=adjacency[,-1] # deleting the first column
adjacencyMatrix=as.matrix(adjacency) 

# the making...
EliteNet=graph_from_adjacency_matrix(adjacencyMatrix,mode="undirected")


summary(EliteNet)
## IGRAPH 156bdc5 UN-- 46 393 -- 
## + attr: name (v/c)

See inside:

V(EliteNet)
## + 46/46 vertices, named, from 156bdc5:
##  [1] rachelerman     mattmcilwain    DaveParkerSEA   toddbishop     
##  [5] ashannstew      LeslieFeinzaig  akipman         matt_oppy      
##  [9] gilbert         juliesandler    BradSmi         crashdev       
## [13] ShaunaCausey    john_gabbert    moniguzman      mattmday       
## [17] Rich_Barton     daryn           lovelletters    etzioni        
## [21] MissDestructo   heatherredman   danshapiro      medinism       
## [25] KieranSnyder    hadip           RajSinghSeattle funcOfJoe      
## [29] kirbywinfield   stevesi         Ryanintheus     sonalpmane     
## [33] SoGulley        2morrowknight   jinman          tarah          
## [37] Jenerationy     lanctot         Kristen_Hammy   nhuntwalker    
## + ... omitted several vertices
E(EliteNet)
## + 393/393 edges from 156bdc5 (vertex names):
##  [1] rachelerman--mattmcilwain    rachelerman--DaveParkerSEA  
##  [3] rachelerman--toddbishop      rachelerman--ashannstew     
##  [5] rachelerman--LeslieFeinzaig  rachelerman--akipman        
##  [7] rachelerman--matt_oppy       rachelerman--gilbert        
##  [9] rachelerman--juliesandler    rachelerman--BradSmi        
## [11] rachelerman--crashdev        rachelerman--ShaunaCausey   
## [13] rachelerman--john_gabbert    rachelerman--moniguzman     
## [15] rachelerman--mattmday        rachelerman--Rich_Barton    
## [17] rachelerman--daryn           rachelerman--lovelletters   
## [19] rachelerman--etzioni         rachelerman--MissDestructo  
## + ... omitted several edges

2. Adding Attributes

Attributes are added using vectors:

# edges
E(EliteNet)$weight=1
# EliteNet=set_edge_attr(EliteNet,"weight", value = 1)

#nodes
V(EliteNet)$male=attributes$male
V(EliteNet)$followers=attributes$followers
# EliteNet=set_vertex_attr(EliteNet, "followers", value=attributes$followers)
##
# E(EliteNet)$weight
# edge_attr_names(EliteNet)
# V(EliteNet)$followers
# vertex_attr_names(EliteNet)
summary(EliteNet)
## IGRAPH 156bdc5 UNW- 46 393 -- 
## + attr: name (v/c), male (v/n), followers (v/n), weight (e/n)

You can use attributes to color nodes:

library(ggraph)

base=ggraph(graph = EliteNet)
base + geom_node_label(aes(label = name,
                           color=as.factor(male)),
                       repel = TRUE,show.legend = F) + 
       geom_edge_link(alpha=0.1) + 
       scale_color_manual(values = c('red','blue'))

III. Exploring the Network

Let’s some key measures that describe the whole network:

  • Connectedness:
is_connected(EliteNet)
## [1] TRUE
  • Density:
edge_density(EliteNet)
## [1] 0.3797101
  • Diameter:
diameter(EliteNet)
## [1] 3
  • Assortativity:
  1. Degree assortativity:
assortativity_degree(EliteNet,directed=F)
## [1] -0.2541114
  1. Categorical assortativity:
assortativity_nominal(EliteNet,as.factor(V(EliteNet)$male),directed = F)
## [1] 0.04310211
  1. Assortativity (numerical):
assortativity(EliteNet,V(EliteNet)$followers,directed = T)
## [1] -0.04515662

IV. Exploration of Network nodes

Let’s compute measures for each node, and save them into a data frame:

eigen=eigen_centrality (EliteNet)$vector

close=closeness(EliteNet,normalized=T)

betw=betweenness(EliteNet,normalized=T)

DFCentrality=as.data.frame(cbind(eigen,close,betw),stringsAsFactors = F)
names(DFCentrality)=c('Eigenvector','Closeness','Betweenness')

DFCentrality$person=row.names(DFCentrality)
row.names(DFCentrality)=NULL

## see
head(DFCentrality)
##   Eigenvector Closeness Betweenness         person
## 1   0.8089981 0.7500000 0.038209947    rachelerman
## 2   0.4840837 0.6000000 0.004696161   mattmcilwain
## 3   0.9154314 0.8333333 0.098119429  DaveParkerSEA
## 4   1.0000000 0.9183673 0.111044213     toddbishop
## 5   0.5803118 0.6250000 0.004575579     ashannstew
## 6   0.5885513 0.6250000 0.003989266 LeslieFeinzaig
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(DFCentrality, aes(x=Betweenness, y=Closeness)) + 
    theme_classic() +
    geom_text(aes(label=person,size=Eigenvector),show.legend = T,alpha=0.5)

The previous results tells us that two people are salient:

HubNodes=dplyr::top_n(DFCentrality,2,Eigenvector)$person
HubNodes
## [1] "DaveParkerSEA" "toddbishop"

Let’s highlight those guys:

NodeCount=length(V(EliteNet))

V(EliteNet)$label=''

for (index in seq(1:NodeCount)){
    currentName=V(EliteNet)$name[index]
    if (currentName%in%HubNodes){
            V(EliteNet)$label[index]=currentName
    }
}

We show here only the hub’s names:

library(ggraph)

base=ggraph(graph = EliteNet)
base  + geom_node_label(aes(label = label), 
                        repel = TRUE,
                        show.legend = F,
                        color='red') + 
         geom_edge_link(alpha=0.1)

5. Communities

5.1 Potential Communities?

  1. A distribution of random network:
# Generate an ensemble of 100 rewired random networks 
set.seed(123)

random_transitivities <- replicate(100, {
  # rewire keeps the degree structure of EliteNet but randomizes edges
  RandomNet <- rewire(EliteNet, each_edge(prob = 1))
  transitivity(RandomNet)
})
  1. The empirical transitivity - our own net
# Calculate your empirical transitivity
empirical_transitivity <- transitivity(EliteNet)
  1. Get Transitivity ratio
# Get the ratio against the AVERAGE of the random networks
ratio <- empirical_transitivity / mean(random_transitivities)
ratio
## [1] 1.369203

As it is greater than 1, we continue:

5.2 Two approaches

  1. Girvan-Newman:

The girvan_newman algorithm is based on creating partitions using the centrality of nodes to create partition too. We can create something similar to the last result like this:

# Membership vector will be selected based 
# on the highest modularity score
partition_girvanNewman_best <- cluster_edge_betweenness(EliteNet)

The best partition was

table(membership(partition_girvanNewman_best))
## 
##  1  2  3  4  5  6  7  8  9 
## 38  1  1  1  1  1  1  1  1

The modularity:

max(partition_girvanNewman_best$modularity)
## [1] 0.004059592
  1. Louvain:
partition_louvain_optimal <- cluster_louvain(EliteNet)

The optimal partition is:

table(membership(partition_louvain_optimal))
## 
##  1  2  3  4  5 
##  8 11 11  6 10

The modularity:

max(partition_louvain_optimal$modularity)
## [1] 0.1095669

5.3 Plotting communities

First, adding the community label to the nodes:

V(EliteNet)$louvain=as.factor(membership(partition_louvain_optimal))

V(EliteNet)$girvanNewman=as.factor(membership(partition_girvanNewman_best))

Now plot the one with the best modularity:

base=ggraph(graph =EliteNet) +
          geom_edge_link(alpha=0.2)

base + geom_node_point(aes(color=louvain), 
                       show.legend = T, size=4)

An alternative:

V(EliteNet)$degree = degree(EliteNet) # first compute node degree and assign

ggraph(EliteNet, layout = 'hive', 
       axis = louvain,
       sort.by = degree
       ) +   
  geom_edge_hive(color='grey80') +   
  geom_axis_hive(aes(colour = louvain), 
                 label = F) + 
  geom_node_point(aes(colour = louvain)) +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), 
                 repel = TRUE, 
                 max.overlaps = 50, # avoid overlap
                 size = 2.5) +
  coord_fixed()

Time to export:

# you need numeric here:
V(EliteNet)$louvain=as.numeric(V(EliteNet)$louvain)
V(EliteNet)$girvanNewman=as.numeric(V(EliteNet)$girvanNewman)
# so this works
write_graph(EliteNet, "EliteNet_R.graphml", "graphml")
